heading: Establishment of flexible marketing allotments
label: 1359cc Establishment of flexible marketing allotments
notes: Codificationand made identical amendments to this section. The amendments by were repealed by .Pub. L. 110–234Pub. L. 110–246Pub. L. 110–234section 4(a) of Pub. L. 110–246

(1), establishment of the overall allotment quantity by deducting from the sum of the estimated sugar consumption and reasonable carryover stocks for the crop year 1,532,000 short tons, raw value, and carry-in stocks of sugar, including sugar in Commodity Credit Corporation inventory; and in par. (2), adjustment of overall allotment quantity to avoid the forfeiture of sugar to the Commodity Credit Corporation.Pub. L. 110–246, § 1403(c)(1)Subsec. (A), inserted subpar. heading, substituted “Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary” for “The Secretary”, and added subpar. (B).Pub. L. 110–246, § 1403(c)(3)Subsec. (h). , struck out subsec. (h). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Whenever the Secretary estimates or reestimates under , or has reason to believe, that imports of sugars, syrups or molasses for human consumption or to be used for the extraction of sugar for human consumption, whether under a tariff-rate quota or in excess or outside of a tariff-rate quota, will exceed 1,532,000 short tons (raw value equivalent) (excluding any imports attributable to reassignment under paragraph (1)(D) or (2)(C) of ), and that the imports would lead to a reduction of the overall allotment quantity, the Secretary shall suspend the marketing allotments established under this section until such time as the imports have been restricted, eliminated, or reduced to or below the level of 1,532,000 short tons (raw value equivalent).”Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish flexible marketing allotments for sugar for any crop year in which the allotments are required under in accordance with this section.section 1359bb(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: sufficient to maintain raw and refined sugar prices above forfeiture levels to avoid forfeiture of sugar to the Commodity Credit Corporation; but

children.label: B
children.text: not less than a quantity equal to 85 percent of the estimated quantity of sugar for domestic human consumption for the crop year.

children.label: A
children.text: raw and refined sugar prices above forfeiture levels to avoid the forfeiture of sugar to the Commodity Credit Corporation; and

children.label: B
children.text: adequate supplies of raw and refined sugar in the domestic market.

children.heading: Marketing allotment for sugar derived from sugar beets and sugar derived from sugarcane
children.label: c Marketing allotment for sugar derived from sugar beets and sugar derived from sugarcane

children.text: sugar derived from sugar beets by establishing a marketing allotment for a crop year at a quantity equal to the product of multiplying the overall allotment quantity for the crop year by 54.35 percent; and
sugar derived from sugarcane by establishing a marketing allotment for a crop year at a quantity equal to the product of multiplying the overall allotment quantity for the crop year by 45.65 percent.

children.heading: Filling cane sugar and beet sugar allotments
children.label: d Filling cane sugar and beet sugar allotments

children.heading: Cane sugar
children.label: 1 Cane sugar
children.text: Each marketing allotment for cane sugar established under this section may only be filled with sugar processed from domestically grown sugarcane.

children.heading: Beet sugar
children.label: 2 Beet sugar
children.text: Each marketing allotment for beet sugar established under this section may only be filled with sugar domestically processed from sugar beets or in-process beet sugar.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The allotment for sugar derived from sugarcane shall be further allotted, among the States in the United States in which sugarcane is produced, after a hearing (if requested by the affected sugarcane processors and growers) and on such notice as the Secretary by regulation may prescribe, in a fair and equitable manner as provided in this subsection and .section 1359dd(b)(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: Collectively
children.label: A Collectively
children.text: Prior to the allotment of sugar derived from sugarcane to any other State, 325,000 short tons, raw value shall be allotted to the offshore States.

children.label: i
children.text: past marketings of sugar, based on the average of the 2 highest years of production of raw cane sugar from the 1996 through 2000 crops;

children.label: ii
children.text: the ability of processors to market the sugar covered under the allotments for the crop year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: past processings of sugar from sugarcane, based on the 3-year average of the 1998 through 2000 crop years.

children.label: A
children.text: past marketings of sugar, based on the average of the 2 highest years of production of raw cane sugar from the 1996 through 2000 crops;

children.label: B
children.text: the ability of processors to market the sugar covered under the allotments for the crop year; and

children.label: C
children.text: past processings of sugar from sugarcane, based on the 3 crop years with the greatest processings (in the mainland States collectively) during the 1991 through 2000 crop years.

children.heading: Filling cane sugar allotments
children.label: f Filling cane sugar allotments
children.text: Except as provided in , a State cane sugar allotment established under subsection (e) for a crop year may be filled only with sugar processed from sugarcane grown in the State covered by the allotment.section 1359ee of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall, based on reestimates under , adjust upward or downward marketing allotments in a fair and equitable manner, as the Secretary determines appropriate, to reflect changes in estimated sugar consumption, stocks, production, or imports.section 1359bb(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: In carrying out subparagraph (A), the Secretary may not reduce the overall allotment quantity to a quantity of less than 85 percent of the estimated quantity of sugar for domestic human consumption for the crop year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), in the case of any increase or decrease in an allotment, each allocation to a processor of the allotment under , and each proportionate share established with respect to the allotment under , shall be increased or decreased by the same percentage that the allotment is increased or decreased.section 1359dd of this titlesection 1359ff(c) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: If the Secretary makes an upward adjustment under paragraph (1)(A), in adjusting allocations among beet sugar processors, the Secretary shall give priority to beet sugar processors with available sugar.

children.heading: Carry-over of reductions
children.label: 3 Carry-over of reductions
children.text: Whenever a marketing allotment for a crop year is required to be reduced during the crop year under this subsection, if, at the time of the reduction, the quantity of sugar marketed exceeds the processor’s reduced allocation, the allocation of an allotment next established for the processor shall be reduced by the quantity of the excess sugar marketed.
